behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module boundary: signature-verification + HTTP handler + queue handoff (MOCK-MISS). A pure unit test of `verifySignature` misses regressions where handler still forwards on auth failure. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; in-process HTTP harness is enough. (b) no AI/ML output. (c) no multi-agentic chain. (d) no LLM-involved orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with real middleware/module wiring and a fake queue sink. Send a request with invalid `X-Signature-256` over an in-process server harness; assert HTTP 401 and zero enqueue calls.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is ordering across handler and queue persistence boundary, not one function. Need real interaction between modules to catch ack-before-persist races. REJECTION-GATE for System: behavior text only requires ordering, not real network/OS effects; an in-process integration with real queue module is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real queue module (local persistence allowed) and instrument enqueue completion vs response send. Assert enqueue commit timestamp/flag occurs before HTTP 200 is emitted.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is decoupling between request path and async processor modules. Unit tests can miss accidental `await` on processor path. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess strictly required; (b) no AI/ML; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + downstream worker trigger in-process. Make worker intentionally block (e.g., promise delayed). Assert response returns immediately after enqueue and before worker completion.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end latency SLO under concurrency and wall-clock constraints; real scheduler/event-loop/network stack behavior can cause failures mocks hide (REAL-INFRA, MOCK-HIDE). Rejection of Integration is disqualified by explicit text: "within 3 seconds wall-clock" and "slow and concurrent".
behaviors.test_description: Run the service as a real process, send concurrent HTTP traffic while prior events are processed slowly, and measure actual response latency. Assert webhook responses stay <3s under specified load profile.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness appears at handoff/state boundary (handler + dedup store/queue). Unit logic tests may miss transactional/idempotency interaction bugs. REJECTION-GATE for System: behavior does not require real OS/network/subprocess; local persistence/module wiring is enough.
behaviors.test_description: Using real dedup/persistence path, submit same event ID twice. Assert first is persisted/enqueued and second is rejected or no-op, with queue containing one copy only.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: This tests mapping of queue backpressure signal to HTTP contract across modules. Unit test of status-mapping helper is insufficient for real handoff failures. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed; (b) no AI/ML; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to queue module/fake that deterministically returns `full`. Send request and assert HTTP 503 plus valid `Retry-After`, and no success enqueue side effects.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This validates real queue implementation with persistence semantics (module + local storage), not a mocked queue. Per level defs, local DB/filesystem behavior is Integration. REJECTION-GATE for System: text requires capacity enforcement, not external network/OS primitives beyond local persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate real persistent queue with a small configured capacity, enqueue until limit, then enqueue one more. Assert overflow write is rejected and persisted state does not exceed capacity.
